commit.message:
fix: Telegram bot improvements — inspect, dedup, context (#54)

* fix: handle Anthropic tool_use finish_reason in agent loop

The agent loop checked for finish_reason == "tool_calls" (OpenAI
format) but Anthropic returns "tool_use". This caused the loop to
break after round 1 without processing tool results, producing
empty responses ("no response generated").

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: add /inspect command to Telegram bot

Shows tool actions, sources, and timing from the last query.
Also captures statuses, sources, and perf_trace events from
the chat SSE stream for inspection.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: prevent duplicate Telegram messages from Claude Code sessions

[NOTIFY] messages were sent during assistant events AND re-extracted
from the result event, causing duplicates. Now skips re-extraction
when notifications were already sent during the session.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: detect imperative follow-ups for conversation context retention

"look in my email", "check my calendar", "search drive for it" etc.
were not recognized as follow-ups, so the previous question's context
was lost. Added regex pattern for imperative verbs + data source nouns.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* test: add tests for PR review findings; strip PII from /inspect

- Add unit tests for expand_followup_query imperative patterns
- Add tests for orchestrator notification dedup (notifications_sent > 0)
- Move _IMPERATIVE_FOLLOWUP_RE to module level per convention
- Strip tool input args from /inspect output (show tool name only)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix(ingest): retry reset when desktop still linked after startup

Initial reset call fails silently when Signal Desktop container isn't
reachable yet. Desktop then comes up still linked from previous session,
and the QR poll loop waits 120s for an unlink that never happens.

fix(telegram): fallback to plain text when Markdown parsing fails

when the API returns "can't parse entities".

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(whatsapp): render QR on server, improve login reliability

- Add silent Baileys logger to suppress noise (no pino dep)
- Use qrcode package to render QR as UTF-8 text on the server and
  return it as qrTerminal in the login/start response
- CLI now prints the server-rendered QR instead of using qrterminal
- Remove qrterminal/rsc.io/qr deps from CLI
- Add 20s QR timeout and explicit close-event rejection in loginStart
- Clear stale auth dir before each new loginStart so a fresh QR is
  always generated
- Add loggingIn flag to suppress auto-reconnect during loginStart
- Increase login/wait timeout from 60s → 5 min to give time to scan
- Add WebSocket error handler for Bun compatibility
- Use fetchLatestBaileysVersion and makeCacheableSignalKeyStore
- Clean up error paths: print styled errors, call waitForEnter, return
  errSilent so the TUI redraws cleanly
- Improve WhatsApp mode option copy for clarity

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

refactor(db): squash 57 migrations into single Init migration

Replace 57 incremental migrations with one squashed Init migration
generated from entity synchronization against a fresh PostgreSQL.

This fixes the CI E2E test failures caused by enum/index duplication
when migrations ran sequentially on a clean database.

Changes:
- Delete 57 old migration files
- Add 1700000000000-Init.ts (full schema: 280 types, 221 tables,
  744 indexes, 196 FKs)
- Fix 7 duplicate @Index() decorators across 4 entity files
  (class-level + property-level on same column)
- Railway typeorm_migrations table updated to reference new Init

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add edit, archive, and delete actions to all boards view and board detail

- All Boards view (grid + list): add Archive item to dropdown, add
  role-based permission guards (canManage/canDelete)
- Board detail context menu: add Edit Board and Archive Board items
  alongside the existing Delete; wire up EditBoardDialog
- Translate remaining English strings in EditBoardDialog
- Add editBoard.description/submit/updating keys to de.ts

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Enforce auth ACL on tunnel channels

Previously, authfile ACL restrictions were only checked during the
initial config handshake. This adds ACL enforcement at the tunnel
layer when processing SSH channel requests, ensuring that each
outbound connection is validated against the user's allowed addresses.

commit.message:
Initial commit: Quantum Multiple Kernel Learning for Financial Classification

Full QMKL pipeline based on IBM/HSBC (2023), ICASSP 2024, and MLPRAE 2025:
- Preprocessing: StandardScaler + PCA + MinMaxScaler [0,2]
- 12 quantum feature maps (Z, ZZ, Pauli variants) with multiple bandwidths
- Fidelity & Projected quantum kernels via Qiskit 1.x
- MKL combination: Average, SDP, Centered Alignment, Projection, Bayesian Opt
- QSVM with precomputed kernel (sklearn)
- Statistical analysis: 20-run evaluation, bootstrap CI, t-test, Wilcoxon
- Ablation studies: n_qubits, bandwidth alpha, n_kernels, kernel type
- 5 Jupyter notebooks + CLI script

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.name: QMKL-Finance
repository.description: Quantum Multiple Kernel Learning for Financial Classification — Qiskit 1.x implementation based on IBM/HSBC (2023)

feat: AI Coach/Student platform — identity verification, topic guard, per-user goals, REST API, proactive messaging, use cases docs

Close remaining gaps for coach/student use cases and document all enabled archetypes:

- HMAC identity verification (widget + REST API senderId spoofing prevention)
- Topic guard pre-filter with tenant overlay merge (CustomizationService.getTopicGuardOverlay)
- Per-user goals (manage_goals scope:"user", SoulLoader read/writeUserGoals)
- REST Chat API (POST /api/v1/chat/:botId) with channel-agnostic pipeline
- Proactive messaging tool (send_proactive_message for Telegram + widget)
- Agent loop user awareness (active users summary in planner prompts)
- Chat history persistence (widget localStorage + GET /api/v1/chat/:botId/history)
- Identity secret rotation endpoint (POST /api/tenants/me/identity-secret/regenerate)
- Use cases documentation page (6 archetypes: Coach, Support, Sales, Onboarding, Knowledge Bot, BaaS)
- Sidebar nav updated in all 12 architecture doc pages
- README updated with AI Coach/Student features section
- 2835 tests pass, 0 fail

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add keyword normalization and bullet relevance reordering

Keyword normalization (_keyword_in_text, _ABBREV_EXPANSIONS):
- Bidirectional abbreviation lookup so "ML" recognises "machine
  learning" and vice-versa (covers ML, NLP, AI, DL, LLM, RAG,
  k8s, CI/CD, BI, SaaS and their expansions)
- Naive plural handling ("APIs" matches "API")
- Used in _assign_keywords_to_bullets (presence check),
  _build_bullet_briefs (present/sibling checks), and
  _score_keyword_fit (word-overlap via canonical expansion)

Bullet reordering (_reorder_bullets_by_relevance):
- After tailoring, sorts bullet pairs (original + suggested) within
  each experience/project/activity by JD keyword match count
- Most JD-relevant bullet moves to position 0, which ATS parsers
  and recruiters read first
- Pairs stay in sync so review UI still shows correct original ↔ suggested

commit.message:
Lesson design quality: mission debrief, scenario prompts, module stacking

- FinalAnswer switches to "Mission Debrief" mode when studentMissions exist:
  header, idle/collecting UI, input spec prompt all reflect mission context
- Landing score API accepts studentMissions and scores each response against
  the student's individual mission question
- Mission selector prompts rewritten as scenarios/challenges instead of
  grammar drills ("You forgot something..." not "Write using 'whereas'")
- 30-minute lessons now generate 4 modules (was 3) for minimum viable arc
- pickBest() uses strongWith metadata for coherent module sequencing
